key_insight: A fully automated OCT deep-learning pipeline can precisely segment retinal atrophy and ellipsoid zone changes to monitor geographic atrophy progression in AMD.
clinical_relevance: This could improve real-world tracking of disease stage and treatment response by providing consistent, high-resolution biomarker measurements from routine OCT scans.
method: Three semantic segmentation models were trained to identify RPE loss, EZ loss/boundaries, and Bruch’s membrane.
The system was developed on 298 diverse SD-OCT volumes spanning multiple AMD phenotypes and healthy eyes.
Performance was validated externally and tested for repeatability, reader agreement, scan density effects, and lesion-size subgroups.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong accuracy and reliability, including Dice scores of 0.88 for RPE loss and 0.87 for EZ loss, Pearson’s r > 0.99, sub-pixel EZ thickness deviation of 2.15 µm, and ICC > 0.98.
watch_item: Generalizability still depends on OCT device consistency, scan protocol quality, and careful validation before broad deployment in different clinical settings.
paper_title: Fully Automated High-Precision Segmentation of Retinal Atrophy and Ellipsoid Zone Thickness in OCT: A Reliable Tool for Real-World GA Monitoring
